definitions.App_Backend: Server-side logic, APIs, databases — role writes code that runs on backend servers. Primary stack is Python/Java/Node/Go/Ruby/Rust on the server.
definitions.App_Frontend: Browser/web UI — role writes code users see and interact with. Primary stack is React/Angular/Vue/Svelte/HTML/CSS/TypeScript.
definitions.App_Fullstack: Both frontend and backend. Role is expected to build features end-to-end, owning both the browser layer and the server layer.
definitions.App_Mobile: Native or cross-platform mobile apps. Primary stack is Swift/Kotlin/React Native/Flutter/Android/iOS.
definitions.App_API_Integration: Builds integrations between systems, third-party APIs, webhooks, ETL for application data, workflow automation.
definitions.App_Desktop: Desktop applications for Windows/macOS/Linux. Electron, Qt, WPF, JavaFX, WinForms.
definitions.App_Embedded: Firmware and embedded systems. C/C++, RTOS, microcontrollers, hardware-software interfaces.
definitions.App_Gaming: Game development. Unity, Unreal Engine, rendering pipelines, game physics, multiplayer networking.

Application Engineering.disambiguation: App_Backend vs App_Fullstack: If JD says 'you will own backend' with no frontend mention → Backend. If it says 'build features end-to-end' or requires both React and a server-side language → Fullstack.

definitions.Data_Engineering: Builds data pipelines, ETL/ELT, data warehouses, data lakes. Tools: Spark, Kafka, Airflow, dbt, Flink.
definitions.Data_Science: Statistical modeling, experimentation, hypothesis testing, Jupyter notebooks, R/Python data analysis.
definitions.ML_Engineering: Productionizes ML models, builds MLOps pipelines, model serving, feature stores. Tools: MLflow, Kubeflow, SageMaker.
definitions.Analytics_BI: Business intelligence, dashboards, reporting. Tools: Tableau, PowerBI, Looker, Redshift, Snowflake.
definitions.Data_Platform: Builds the data infrastructure others use — metadata management, data catalog, data quality, governance frameworks.

Data & Intelligence.disambiguation: Data_Engineering vs ML_Engineering: If the role is mostly about moving data → Engineering. If deploying ML models in production → ML Engineering.

definitions.Cloud_Infrastructure: Manages cloud accounts, VPCs, IAM, security groups, provisioning. AWS/Azure/GCP administration at organization scale.
definitions.DevOps: Builds and manages CI/CD pipelines, container orchestration, release management. Responsible for developer productivity at scale.
definitions.SRE: Reliability engineering — SLOs/SLIs, incident response, on-call, chaos engineering, performance optimization.
definitions.Network_Engineering: Network topology, routing, firewalls, VPNs, load balancers, DNS. Cisco/Juniper or cloud networking.
definitions.Database_Administration: Manages database infrastructure — backups, replication, query performance, schema migrations, HA/DR setup.

Infrastructure & Cloud.disambiguation: DevOps vs SRE: DevOps owns CI/CD and deployment pipelines. SRE owns reliability metrics and incident response.

definitions.Security_Engineering: Builds security tooling, secure code review, SAST/DAST integration, vulnerability management.
definitions.Pen_Testing: Offensive security — penetration testing, red teaming, exploit development, bug bounty.
definitions.SOC_Operations: Security operations center — threat detection, SIEM, incident response, log analysis.
definitions.GRC_Compliance: Governance, risk, compliance — SOC2, ISO 27001, HIPAA, GDPR, audit management.
definitions.IAM: Identity and access management — SSO, OAuth, SAML, LDAP, role-based access control, directory services.

definitions.Developer_Platform: Internal developer portals, self-service scaffolding, infrastructure-as-code templates, developer experience.
definitions.Observability_Tooling: Builds monitoring, logging, tracing infrastructure. Prometheus, Grafana, ELK, Datadog.
definitions.API_Gateway: Manages API gateways, rate limiting, API versioning, developer portals for external APIs.
definitions.Auth_Platform: Manages authentication and authorization platform — identity providers, token management, MFA.

definitions.Product_Management: Defines product roadmap, writes PRDs, coordinates engineering, manages stakeholders, tracks OKRs.
definitions.UX_Design: User experience design — wireframes, prototypes, design systems, Figma, accessibility.
definitions.Product_Design: Visual and interaction design — branding, motion graphics, component libraries.
definitions.User_Research: Qualitative and quantitative user research — interviews, usability testing, surveys, personas.
